name: EOSINOPHILIC GASTROENTEROPATHY
definition: manifestation of immune-mediated inflammation (allergy) in genetically susceptible uncommon occasionally self-limited form of gastroenteritis with remissions + non-IgE-mediated allergic disease

demographics.Histo: PATHOGNOMONIC infiltration of squamous epithelium with > 15-20 eosinophils per

discriminating_features: smooth long-segment stricture without ulceration + mild mucosal irregularity (most
"ringed esophagus" = multiple fixed rings appearing as closely stacked indentations on
stricture with an irregular ulcerated lumen / granular mucosa

general: smooth long-segment stricture without ulceration + mild mucosal irregularity (most
"ringed esophagus" = multiple fixed rings appearing as closely stacked indentations on
stricture with an irregular ulcerated lumen / granular mucosa
Small bowel: mucosal fold thickening, polyps, nodular thickening, ulcers
"araneid limblike" sign = "spider leg" appearance ← diffuse mucosal thickening with
"halo" sign = layered appearance of bowel wall ← submucosal edema
strictures, bowel wall thickening, intestinal obstruction
Stomach (80%; almost always limited to a: enlarged gastric rugae / cobblestone nodules / polyps
thickened + rigid wall with narrowed gastric antrum / pylorus
bulky intraluminal mass up to 9 cm in size
segmental / diffuse colonic wall thickening, wall edema

clinical.Prognosis: tendency toward spontaneous remission (in ⅓) with frequent relapse
clinical.Rx: corticosteroids / removal of sensitizing agent; dietary modification
